zh.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon是Herfast de Crépon的兒子，於大約0990年出生在法國巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯可雷朋。他於大約1009年與Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux在法國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1040年在法國豪-諾門迪艾勒 瓦德易爾享年52歲，埋葬在法國。

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Steward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

variants.name: Stuard
meanings: Variant of   Steward  .

variants.name: Stewart
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Crépon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English or Scottish: unexplained. Compare   Creason  .

variants.name: Creason
meanings: English or Scottish: unexplained. Compare   Creson  .
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Dutch Cruijsen, or one of its variants such as Kruijssen or Creusen, habitational names based on Dutch kruis ‘cross’ (compare Crusan and Kruis ).

nameMeanings.name: Osbern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Old English personal name composed of ōs ‘god’ + beorn ‘bear, warrior’ (both of Scandinavian origin). As a modern given name it generally represents a transferred use of the surname that was derived from this name during the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Osbourne
meanings: From a Late Old English personal name composed of ōs ‘god’ + beorn ‘bear, warrior’ (both of Scandinavian origin). As a modern given name it generally represents a transferred use of the surname that was derived from this name during the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Osborne
meanings: From a Late Old English personal name composed of ōs ‘god’ + beorn ‘bear, warrior’ (both of Scandinavian origin). As a modern given name it generally represents a transferred use of the surname that was derived from this name during the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon was born about 0990, in Crépon, Calvados, Lower Normandy, France as the son of Herfast de Crépon. He married Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux about 1009, in France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died about 1040, in Le Vaudreuil, Eure, Upper Normandy, France, at the age of 52, and was buried in France.

fr.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon est né environ 0990, à Crépon, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, France, fils de Herfast de Crépon. Il a épousé Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux environ 1009, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé environ 1040, à Le Vaudreuil, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à France.

de.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon wurde um 0990 in Crépon, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich, als Sohn von Herfast de Crépon geboren. Er hat um 1009 in Frankreich Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb um 1040 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Le Vaudreuil, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon è nato il circa 0990 nel Crépon, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia come figlio di Herfast de Crépon. Lui sposato Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux circa 1009, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì circa 1040, nel Le Vaudreuil, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Créponは約0990年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディカルヴァドスクルeプオンでHerfast de Créponの息子として生まれた。約1009年にフランスでEmma d’Ivry et de Bayeuxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は約1040年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアル ヴァウドレウイルで52歳で死去し，フランスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon(은)는 대략 0990년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 캘바도스 크e폰에서 Herfast de Crépon의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1009년 프랑스에서 Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1040년 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르 일 바드레우일에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon nasceu aproximadamente 0990, em Crépon, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, França filho de Herfast de Crépon. Ele casou-se com Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux aproximadamente 1009, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1040, em Le Vaudreuil, Eure, Haute-Normandie, França, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em França.

ru.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon родился около 0990 г. в местности крeпон, калвадос, бассе-норманди, Франция. Его отца звали Herfast de Crépon. Он женился на женщине по имени Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux около 1009 г. в местности Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер около 1040 г. в местности л ваудреуил, эур, хауте-норманди, Франция в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Osbern the Steward de Crépon nació aproximadamente 0990, en Crépon, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia como hijo de Herfast de Crépon. Se casó con Emma d’Ivry et de Bayeux aproximadamente 1009, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1040, en Le Vaudreuil, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Francia.
